title: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript
description: Complete API reference for the TypeScript Agent SDK, including all functions, types, and interfaces.

text: Learn more about the TypeScript V2 preview
Try the new V2 interface (preview): A simplified interface with `send()` and `stream()` patterns is now available, making multi-turn conversations easier. Learn more about the TypeScript V2 preview

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Overview
text: > Complete API reference for the TypeScript Agent SDK, including all functions, types, and interfaces.
npm install @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
`pathToClaudeCodeExecutable`

The SDK bundles a native Claude Code binary for your platform as an optional dependency such as `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk-darwin-arm64`. You don't need to install Claude Code separately. If your package manager skips optional dependencies, the SDK throws `Native CLI binary for <platform> not found`; set `pathToClaudeCodeExecutable` to a separately installed `claude` binary instead.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Functions > `query()`
text: The primary function for interacting with Claude Code. Creates an async generator that streams messages as they arrive.
`WarmQuery`

preceding_paragraph: Pre-warms the CLI subprocess by spawning it and completing the initialize handshake before a prompt is available. The returned `WarmQuery` handle accepts a prompt later and writes it to an already-ready process, so the first `query()` call resolves without paying subprocess spawn and initialization cost inline.

text: `Options`

cells: Optional configuration object. Same as the `options` parameter to `query()`
Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for subprocess initialization. Defaults to `60000`. If initialization does not complete in time, the promise rejects with a timeout error

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Functions > `startup()` > Parameters

text: `WarmQuery`

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Functions > `startup()` > Returns
text: Returns a `Promise<``WarmQuery``>` that resolves once the subprocess has spawned and completed its initialize handshake.

preceding_paragraph: Call `startup()` early, for example on application boot, then call `.query()` on the returned handle once a prompt is ready. This moves subprocess spawn and initialization out of the critical path.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Functions > `startup()` > Example
text: Call `startup()` early, for example on application boot, then call `.query()` on the returned handle once a prompt is ready. This moves subprocess spawn and initialization out of the critical path.

text: Pre-warms the CLI subprocess by spawning it and completing the initialize handshake before a prompt is available. The returned `WarmQuery` handle accepts a prompt later and writes it to an already-ready process, so the first `query()` call resolves without paying subprocess spawn and initialization cost inline.

cells: Agent name for the main thread. The agent must be defined in the `agents` option or in settings
Enable bypassing permissions. Required when using `permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions'`

Tools to auto-approve without prompting. This does not restrict Claude to only these tools; unlisted tools fall through to `permissionMode` and `canUseTool`. Use `disallowedTools` to block tools. See Permissions
Custom permission function for tool usage
Enable debug mode for the Claude Code process
Write debug logs to a specific file path. Implicitly enables debug mode
Tools to always deny. Deny rules are checked first and override `allowedTools` and `permissionMode` (including `bypassPermissions`)
Controls how much effort Claude puts into its response. Works with adaptive thinking to guide thinking depth
Enable file change tracking for rewinding. See File checkpointing
Environment variables. See Environment variables for variables the underlying CLI reads. Set `CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_CLIENT_APP` to identify your app in the User-Agent header
Arguments to pass to the executable
Model to use if primary fails
When resuming with `resume`, fork to a new session ID instead of continuing the original session
Stop the query when the client-side cost estimate reaches this USD value. Compared against the same estimate as `total_cost_usd`; see Track cost and usage for accuracy caveats
Deprecated: Use `thinking` instead. Maximum tokens for thinking process
Define output format for agent results. See Structured outputs for details
Path to Claude Code executable. Only needed if optional dependencies were skipped during install or your platform isn't in the supported set
MCP tool name for permission prompts
When `false`, disables session persistence to disk. Sessions cannot be resumed later
Load custom plugins from local paths. See Plugins for details
Enable prompt suggestions. Emits a `prompt_suggestion` message after each turn with a predicted next user prompt
Resume session at a specific message UUID
Configure sandbox behavior programmatically. See Sandbox settings for details
Use a specific UUID for the session instead of auto-generating one
Mirror session transcripts to an external backend so any host can resume them. See Persist sessions to external storage
Control which filesystem settings to load. Pass `[]` to disable user, project, and local settings. Managed policy settings load regardless. See Use Claude Code features
Custom function to spawn the Claude Code process. Use to run Claude Code in VMs, containers, or remote environments
`string | { type: 'preset'; preset: 'claude_code'; append?: string; excludeDynamicSections?: boolean }`

System prompt configuration. Pass a string for custom prompt, or `{ type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' }` to use Claude Code's system prompt. When using the preset object form, add `append` to extend it with additional instructions, and set `excludeDynamicSections: true` to move per-session context into the first user message for better prompt-cache reuse across machines
Controls Claude's thinking/reasoning behavior. See `ThinkingConfig` for options
Configuration for built-in tool behavior. See `ToolConfig` for details
Tool configuration. Pass an array of tool names or use the preset to get Claude Code's default tools

cells: Interrupts the query (only available in streaming input mode)

Changes the permission mode (only available in streaming input mode)
Changes the model (only available in streaming input mode)
Deprecated: Use the `thinking` option instead. Changes the maximum thinking tokens
Returns the full initialization result including supported commands, models, account info, and output style configuration
Returns available models with display info
Returns status of connected MCP servers
Reconnect an MCP server by name
Enable or disable an MCP server by name
Dynamically replace the set of MCP servers for this session. Returns info about which servers were added, removed, and any errors
Stream input messages to the query for multi-turn conversations
Stop a running background task by ID
Close the query and terminate the underlying process. Forcefully ends the query and cleans up all resources

text: Interface returned by the `query()` function.
`startup()`

preceding_paragraph: Handle returned by `startup()`. The subprocess is already spawned and initialized, so calling `query()` on this handle writes the prompt directly to a ready process with no startup latency.

cells: Send a prompt to the pre-warmed subprocess and return a `Query`. Can only be called once per `WarmQuery`
Close the subprocess without sending a prompt. Use this to discard a warm query that is no longer needed

cells: Natural language description of when to use this agent

Array of tool names to explicitly disallow for this agent
Model override for this agent. Accepts an alias such as `'sonnet'`, `'opus'`, `'haiku'`, `'inherit'`, or a full model ID. If omitted or `'inherit'`, uses the main model
MCP server specifications for this agent
Array of skill names to preload into the agent context

Maximum number of agentic turns (API round-trips) before stopping
Run this agent as a non-blocking background task when invoked
Memory source for this agent: `'user'`, `'project'`, or `'local'`
Reasoning effort level for this agent. Accepts a named level or an integer
Permission mode for tool execution within this agent. See `PermissionMode`
Experimental: Critical reminder added to the system prompt

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Types > `AgentDefinition`
text: Configuration for a subagent defined programmatically.
type AgentMcpServerSpec = string | Record<string, McpServerConfigForProcessTransport>;
preceding_paragraph: Specifies MCP servers available to a subagent. Can be a server name (string referencing a server from the parent's `mcpServers` config) or an inline server configuration record mapping server names to configs.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Types > `AgentMcpServerSpec`
text:
Specifies MCP servers available to a subagent. Can be a server name (string referencing a server from the parent's `mcpServers` config) or an inline server configuration record mapping server names to configs.

Where `McpServerConfigForProcessTransport` is `McpStdioServerConfig | McpSSEServerConfig | McpHttpServerConfig | McpSdkServerConfig`.
type SettingSource = "user" | "project" | "local";
preceding_paragraph: Controls which filesystem-based configuration sources the SDK loads settings from.

text: What settingSources does not control

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Types > `SettingSource` > Default behavior
text: When `settingSources` is omitted or `undefined`, `query()` loads the same filesystem settings as the Claude Code CLI: user, project, and local. Managed policy settings are loaded in all cases. See What settingSources does not control for inputs that are read regardless of this option, and how to disable them.
CLAUDE.md

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Types > `SettingSource` > Why use settingSources
text:
Disable filesystem settings:

Load all filesystem settings explicitly:

Load only specific setting sources:

Testing and CI environments:

Loading CLAUDE.md project instructions:

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Types > `SettingSource` > Settings precedence
text:
When multiple sources are loaded, settings are merged with this precedence (highest to lowest):

1. Local settings (`.claude/settings.local.json`)
2. Project settings (`.claude/settings.json`)
3. User settings (`~/.claude/settings.json`)

Programmatic options such as `agents` and `allowedTools` override user, project, and local filesystem settings. Managed policy settings take precedence over programmatic options.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Types > `SettingSource`
text: Controls which filesystem-based configuration sources the SDK loads settings from.

type PermissionMode =
  | "default" // Standard permission behavior
  | "acceptEdits" // Auto-accept file edits
  | "bypassPermissions" // Bypass all permission checks
  | "plan" // Planning mode - no execution
  | "dontAsk" // Don't prompt for permissions, deny if not pre-approved
  | "auto"; // Use a model classifier to approve or deny each tool call

type SDKMessage =
  | SDKAssistantMessage
  | SDKUserMessage
  | SDKUserMessageReplay
  | SDKResultMessage
  | SDKSystemMessage
  | SDKPartialAssistantMessage
  | SDKCompactBoundaryMessage
  | SDKStatusMessage
  | SDKLocalCommandOutputMessage
  | SDKHookStartedMessage
  | SDKHookProgressMessage
  | SDKHookResponseMessage
  | SDKPluginInstallMessage
  | SDKToolProgressMessage
  | SDKAuthStatusMessage
  | SDKTaskNotificationMessage
  | SDKTaskStartedMessage
  | SDKTaskProgressMessage
  | SDKTaskUpdatedMessage
  | SDKFilesPersistedEvent
  | SDKToolUseSummaryMessage
  | SDKRateLimitEvent
  | SDKPromptSuggestionMessage;
preceding_paragraph: Union type of all possible messages returned by the query.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Message Types > `SDKMessage`
text: Union type of all possible messages returned by the query.
`BetaMessage`

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Message Types > `SDKAssistantMessage`
text:
Assistant response message.

The `message` field is a `BetaMessage` from the Anthropic SDK. It includes fields like `id`, `content`, `model`, `stop_reason`, and `usage`.

`SDKAssistantMessageError` is one of: `'authentication_failed'`, `'billing_error'`, `'rate_limit'`, `'invalid_request'`, `'server_error'`, `'max_output_tokens'`, or `'unknown'`.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Message Types > `SDKUserMessage`
text:
User input message.

Set `shouldQuery` to `false` to append the message to the transcript without triggering an assistant turn. The message is held and merged into the next user message that does trigger a turn. Use this to inject context, such as the output of a command you ran out of band, without spending a model call on it.

preceding_paragraph: Replayed user message with required UUID.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Message Types > `SDKUserMessageReplay`
text: Replayed user message with required UUID.
Defer a tool call for later

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Message Types > `SDKResultMessage`
text:
Final result message.

When a `PreToolUse` hook returns `permissionDecision: "defer"`, the result has `stop_reason: "tool_deferred"` and `deferred_tool_use` carries the pending tool's `id`, `name`, and `input`. Read this field to surface the request in your own UI, then resume with the same `session_id` to continue. See Defer a tool call for later for the full round trip.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Message Types > `SDKSystemMessage`
text: System initialization message.

preceding_paragraph: Streaming partial message (only when `includePartialMessages` is true).

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Message Types > `SDKPartialAssistantMessage`
text: Streaming partial message (only when `includePartialMessages` is true).

preceding_paragraph: Message indicating a conversation compaction boundary.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Message Types > `SDKCompactBoundaryMessage`
text: Message indicating a conversation compaction boundary.
`CLAUDE_CODE_SYNC_PLUGIN_INSTALL`

preceding_paragraph: Plugin installation progress event. Emitted when `CLAUDE_CODE_SYNC_PLUGIN_INSTALL` is set, so your Agent SDK application can track marketplace plugin installation before the first turn. The `started` and `completed` statuses bracket the overall install. The `installed` and `failed` statuses report individual marketplaces and include `name`.

text: Plugin installation progress event. Emitted when `CLAUDE_CODE_SYNC_PLUGIN_INSTALL` is set, so your Agent SDK application can track marketplace plugin installation before the first turn. The `started` and `completed` statuses bracket the overall install. The `installed` and `failed` statuses report individual marketplaces and include `name`.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > Agent
text:
Tool name: `Agent` (previously `Task`, which is still accepted as an alias)

Launches a new agent to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously.
Handle approvals and user input

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > AskUserQuestion
text:
Tool name: `AskUserQuestion`

Asks the user clarifying questions during execution. See Handle approvals and user input for usage details.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > Bash
text:
Tool name: `Bash`

Executes bash commands in a persistent shell session with optional timeout and background execution.
Monitor tool reference

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > Monitor
text:
Tool name: `Monitor`

Runs a background script and delivers each stdout line to Claude as an event so it can react without polling. Set `persistent: true` for session-length watches such as log tails. Monitor follows the same permission rules as Bash. See the Monitor tool reference for behavior and provider availability.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > TaskOutput
text:
Tool name: `TaskOutput`

Retrieves output from a running or completed background task.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > Edit
text:
Tool name: `Edit`

Performs exact string replacements in files.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > Read
text:
Tool name: `Read`

Reads files from the local filesystem, including text, images, PDFs, and Jupyter notebooks. Use `pages` for PDF page ranges (for example, `"1-5"`).

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > Write
text:
Tool name: `Write`

Writes a file to the local filesystem, overwriting if it exists.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > Glob
text:
Tool name: `Glob`

Fast file pattern matching that works with any codebase size.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > Grep
text:
Tool name: `Grep`

Powerful search tool built on ripgrep with regex support.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > TaskStop
text:
Tool name: `TaskStop`

Stops a running background task or shell by ID.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > NotebookEdit
text:
Tool name: `NotebookEdit`

Edits cells in Jupyter notebook files.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > WebFetch
text:
Tool name: `WebFetch`

Fetches content from a URL and processes it with an AI model.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > WebSearch
text:
Tool name: `WebSearch`

Searches the web and returns formatted results.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > TodoWrite
text:
Tool name: `TodoWrite`

Creates and manages a structured task list for tracking progress.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > ExitPlanMode
text:
Tool name: `ExitPlanMode`

Exits planning mode. Optionally specifies prompt-based permissions needed to implement the plan.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > ListMcpResources
text:
Tool name: `ListMcpResources`

Lists available MCP resources from connected servers.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > ReadMcpResource
text:
Tool name: `ReadMcpResource`

Reads a specific MCP resource from a server.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types > EnterWorktree
text:
Tool name: `EnterWorktree`

Creates and enters a temporary git worktree for isolated work. Pass `path` to switch into an existing worktree of the current repository instead of creating a new one. `name` and `path` are mutually exclusive.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Input Types
text: Documentation of input schemas for all built-in Claude Code tools. These types are exported from `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` and can be used for type-safe tool interactions.

type ToolOutputSchemas =
  | AgentOutput
  | AskUserQuestionOutput
  | BashOutput
  | EnterWorktreeOutput
  | ExitPlanModeOutput
  | FileEditOutput
  | FileReadOutput
  | FileWriteOutput
  | GlobOutput
  | GrepOutput
  | ListMcpResourcesOutput
  | MonitorOutput
  | NotebookEditOutput
  | ReadMcpResourceOutput
  | TaskStopOutput
  | TodoWriteOutput
  | WebFetchOutput
  | WebSearchOutput;
preceding_paragraph: Union of all tool output types.

text: Union of all tool output types.

preceding_paragraph: Tool name: `Agent` (previously `Task`, which is still accepted as an alias)

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > Agent
text:
Tool name: `Agent` (previously `Task`, which is still accepted as an alias)

Returns the result from the subagent. Discriminated on the `status` field: `"completed"` for finished tasks, `"async_launched"` for background tasks, and `"sub_agent_entered"` for interactive subagents.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > AskUserQuestion
text:
Tool name: `AskUserQuestion`

Returns the questions asked and the user's answers.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > Bash
text:
Tool name: `Bash`

Returns command output with stdout/stderr split. Background commands include a `backgroundTaskId`.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > Monitor
text:
Tool name: `Monitor`

Returns the background task ID for the running monitor. Use this ID with `TaskStop` to cancel the watch early.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > Edit
text:
Tool name: `Edit`

Returns the structured diff of the edit operation.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > Read
text:
Tool name: `Read`

Returns file contents in a format appropriate to the file type. Discriminated on the `type` field.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > Write
text:
Tool name: `Write`

Returns the write result with structured diff information.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > Glob
text:
Tool name: `Glob`

Returns file paths matching the glob pattern, sorted by modification time.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > Grep
text:
Tool name: `Grep`

Returns search results. The shape varies by `mode`: file list, content with matches, or match counts.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > TaskStop
text:
Tool name: `TaskStop`

Returns confirmation after stopping the background task.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > NotebookEdit
text:
Tool name: `NotebookEdit`

Returns the result of the notebook edit with original and updated file contents.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > WebFetch
text:
Tool name: `WebFetch`

Returns the fetched content with HTTP status and metadata.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > WebSearch
text:
Tool name: `WebSearch`

Returns search results from the web.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > TodoWrite
text:
Tool name: `TodoWrite`

Returns the previous and updated task lists.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > ExitPlanMode
text:
Tool name: `ExitPlanMode`

Returns the plan state after exiting plan mode.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Tool Output Types > ListMcpResources

Returns an array of available MCP resources.

Returns the contents of the requested MCP resource.

text: Beta headers
type SdkBeta = "context-1m-2025-08-07";
preceding_paragraph: Available beta features that can be enabled via the `betas` option. See Beta headers for more information.

text: Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, or Claude Opus 4.7

The `context-1m-2025-08-07` beta is retired as of April 30, 2026. Passing this value with Claude Sonnet 4.5 or Sonnet 4 has no effect, and requests that exceed the standard 200k-token context window return an error. To use a 1M-token context window, migrate to Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, or Claude Opus 4.7, which include 1M context at standard pricing with no beta header required.

preceding_paragraph: Configuration for sandbox behavior. Use this to enable command sandboxing and configure network restrictions programmatically.

text: permissions system
`SandboxNetworkConfig`
`SandboxFilesystemConfig`

cells: Enable sandbox mode for command execution
Auto-approve bash commands when sandbox is enabled
Commands that always bypass sandbox restrictions (e.g., `['docker']`). These run unsandboxed automatically without model involvement
Allow the model to request running commands outside the sandbox. When `true`, the model can set `dangerouslyDisableSandbox` in tool input, which falls back to the permissions system
Filesystem-specific sandbox configuration for read/write restrictions
Map of violation categories to patterns to ignore (e.g., `{ file: ['/tmp/*'], network: ['localhost'] }`)
Enable a weaker nested sandbox for compatibility
Custom ripgrep binary configuration for sandbox environments

text: Unix socket security: The `allowUnixSockets` option can grant access to powerful system services. For example, allowing `/var/run/docker.sock` effectively grants full host system access through the Docker API, bypassing sandbox isolation. Only allow Unix sockets that are strictly necessary and understand the security implications of each.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Sandbox Configuration > `SandboxSettings` > Example usage
Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Sandbox Configuration > `SandboxSettings`
text: Configuration for sandbox behavior. Use this to enable command sandboxing and configure network restrictions programmatically.

cells: Domain names that sandboxed processes can access
Domain names that sandboxed processes cannot access. Takes precedence over `allowedDomains`
Restrict network access to only the domains in `allowedDomains`
Allow processes to bind to local ports (e.g., for dev servers)
Unix socket paths that processes can access (e.g., Docker socket)
Allow access to all Unix sockets
HTTP proxy port for network requests
SOCKS proxy port for network requests

text: domain fronting
Sandboxing security limitations
Secure deployment

The built-in sandbox proxy enforces `allowedDomains` based on the requested hostname and does not terminate or inspect TLS traffic, so techniques such as domain fronting can potentially bypass it. See Sandboxing security limitations for details and Secure deployment for configuring a TLS-terminating proxy.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Sandbox Configuration > `SandboxNetworkConfig`
text: Network-specific configuration for sandbox mode.

cells: File path patterns to allow write access to
File path patterns to deny write access to
File path patterns to deny read access to

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Sandbox Configuration > `SandboxFilesystemConfig`
text: Filesystem-specific configuration for sandbox mode.

`excludedCommands` vs `allowUnsandboxedCommands`:

- `excludedCommands`: A static list of commands that always bypass the sandbox automatically (e.g., `['docker']`). The model has no control over this.
- `allowUnsandboxedCommands`: Lets the model decide at runtime whether to request unsandboxed execution by setting `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` in the tool input.

preceding_paragraph: When `allowUnsandboxedCommands` is enabled, the model can request to run commands outside the sandbox by setting `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` in the tool input. These requests fall back to the existing permissions system, meaning your `canUseTool` handler is invoked, allowing you to implement custom authorization logic.

text:
Commands running with `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` have full system access. Ensure your `canUseTool` handler validates these requests carefully.

If `permissionMode` is set to `bypassPermissions` and `allowUnsandboxedCommands` is enabled, the model can autonomously execute commands outside the sandbox without any approval prompts. This combination effectively allows the model to escape sandbox isolation silently.

breadcrumb: Agent SDK reference - TypeScript > Sandbox Configuration > Permissions Fallback for Unsandboxed Commands
text:
When `allowUnsandboxedCommands` is enabled, the model can request to run commands outside the sandbox by setting `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` in the tool input. These requests fall back to the existing permissions system, meaning your `canUseTool` handler is invoked, allowing you to implement custom authorization logic.

This pattern enables you to:

- Audit model requests: Log when the model requests unsandboxed execution
- Implement allowlists: Only permit specific commands to run unsandboxed
- Add approval workflows: Require explicit authorization for privileged operations
